channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Trump turned the TikTok ban into a political asset
item.description: President Trump has managed to turn a controversial TikTok ban into a political advantage, framing himself as the savior of the popular video-sharing platform by offering the app a 75-day reprieve.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Texas Bans Chinese AI, Social Media Apps From Government Devices
item.description: Texas Governor Greg Abbott bans Chinese AI company DeepSeek and social media apps Xiaohongshu and Lemon8 from government devices due to security concerns.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How AI can drive business transformation
item.description: The race to implement artificial intelligence solutions across the enterprise is in full swing. Most are using AI to drive down bottom-line costs by doing things quicker or more frequently than they did before.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Are you problematic? This ‘cancel culture insurance’ could help you deal with social media backlash
item.description: Worried about getting canceled? There’s now insurance policy for that. Insurance agency Samphire Risk is rolling out “cancel culture” insurance for celebrities and execs afraid of social media backlash, according to a report from the Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: President Trump to meet with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is meeting Friday with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, whose company designs and supplies the advanced computer chips that play an integral role in developing artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Kevin Freking And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Disability monitoring tool leaked personal information online
item.description: AngelSense was leaking names, location data, and more to anyone with a browser and knowledge of the right IP address.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: VMware offers fixes to severe vulnerabilities in VMware Aria
item.description: VMware has fixed multiple high-severity vulnerabilities affecting its cloud management platform (CMP), VMware Aria, which could allow attackers to steal sensitive credentials from the virtualization giant’s IT management and logging solutions.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Opinion: AI is harming our children. California must step up
item.description: Will lawmakers again permit another addictive technology open to children and stand by as another generation gets hurt?
item.dc:creator: Robert C. Fellmeth, The Mercury News

item.title: UPS is hugely scaling back its Amazon shipping deal
item.description: UPS has confirmed plans to reduce its contract with Amazon by more than 50% by the second half of 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Purchasing the future you want
item.description: Procurement and sustainability professionals recognise the vital role sustainable procurement plays in “purchasing the future we want.” There has never been a more pressing set of indicators demonstrating the scale of the issues facing humans and the planet.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bringing zero downtime to anywhere work
item.description: In a work-from-anywhere world, how can a team of so few be expected to keep doing so much for so many? The pace is unsustainable. It’s time for IT to enlist some support of its own and delegate—handing off time-consuming tasks to people and to intelligent, automated tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: RFK Jr. kept asking to see the science that vaccines were safe. After he saw it, he dismissed it
item.description: The man who hopes to be President Donald Trump’s health secretary says he needs to see data showing vaccines are safe, but he dismissed the evidence shown by an influential Republican senator.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: John Mulaney dunked on Meghan Markle and Prince Harry at star-studded Netflix event
item.description: The comedian poked fun at Meghan and Harry's Netflix track record, shortly after the duchess announced she was delaying the release of her lifestyle show in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Italy blocks access to the Chinese AI application DeepSeek to protect users' data
item.description: Italy’s data protection authority has blocked use of Chinese tech startup DeepSeek’s AI application to protect Italians’ data and announced an investigation into the companies behind the chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco touts ‘Internet of Agents’ for secure AI agent collaboration
item.description: When AI agents begin to proliferate, a new, open structure will be needed so they can securely communicate and collaborate together to solve complex problems, suggests Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA unveils AI Essentials training resource
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing how work gets done, creating new technology roles, and transforming the entire employment landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Larry Magid: Testing DeepSeek, the Chinese generative AI app
item.description: Based on my tests and published reports, DeepSeek not yet as advanced as its American counterparts, but it’s quite good
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Mattermost’s Bill Anderson, Matthew Heideman on how DoD benefits from platform approach to collaboration
item.description: Mattermost’s tech experts explain how they create the “connective tissues of information, people and data” for this secure collaboration platform.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Warehouse robot uses AI to play real-life Tetris to handle more than ever before
item.description: A multipurpose robotic system promises to transform how items are stacked onto pallets, aiming to maximize space utilization and reduce shipping costs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How execs can bridge the AI knowledge gap
item.description: From streamlining administrative tasks to enhancing brainstorming sessions, AI is becoming an essential workplace companion. Yet, despite its transformative promise, its integration isn’t as simple as flipping a switch.
item.dc:creator: Dave Grow, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Most in new poll disapprove of Musk's role in Trump administration
item.description: Most registered voters disapprove of tech billionaire Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration, according to a poll released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sophisticated Google Calendar Phishing Campaign Uncovered
item.description: A sophisticated phishing campaign exploiting Google Calendar has been uncovered by Check Point Software Technologies, raising alarms among cybersecurity experts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Satya Nadella is weathering the storm of Trump’s first days
item.description: It’s going to be a long four years for Microsoft as the company tries to navigate the stormy chaos and pledges of retribution that follow wherever President Donald J. Trump’s mind wanders.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system
item.description: The plaintiffs claim OPM violated the E-Government Act by not releasing details of how the email system will manage federal employees’ personal information. The post Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The crisis of AI’s hidden costs
item.description: The AI gold rush is creating an unexpected problem: massive waste in resource provisioning. Cloud cost overruns inevitably lead to a lack of true ROI for these systems. Many enterprises spend $2 to get $1 of benefits. That dog won’t hunt.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A busy stock market week bookended by DeepSeek and Trump tariffs, by the numbers
item.description: News from China upset U.S. markets Monday and disrupted the frenzy that had built up around artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Outsmart hackers who are out to steal your identity
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers tips on how to protect your identity: Be vigilant, monitor accounts, use smart security, know theft response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
